title: coronilla varia subsp. hirta
intro: Coronilla varia subsp. hirta, also known as hairy crown vetch, is a perennial legume plant native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Coronilla varia subsp. hirta include reducing inflammation and pain, as well as its potential to improve cardiovascular health and immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and its flowers are used to make a medicinal extract.
